text: The orifice is visible in blue, indicating its location within the surgical field.
The surgeon uses diathermy to prepare the rectovaginal septum around the orifice, creating a clear area for subsequent suturing.
The surgical team continues the procedure in a cephalad direction, progressing upwards from the previously prepared rectovaginal septum.
The surgeon uses robotic bipolar forceps to gently lift the rectal wall, providing a clear view of the underlying tissue layers.
The surgeon develops the dissection plane laterally, carefully dissecting the distal portion to separate the rectum from the vagina.
The surgeon displays the orifice in the vagina, highlighted in red. Single stitches are used to repair and close the vaginal wall.
The surgeon completes the suturing of the rectovaginal septum, securing the tissue around the orifice with single stitches.
A biologic mesh is placed over the vaginal suture, covering the repaired area of the vaginal wall.
The surgeon uses a V-lock stitch to close the rectum with a running suture, ensuring the tissue edges are approximated securely.
The final result of the surgical procedure is displayed, showing the completed repair with the biologic mesh covering the vaginal wall and the rectum closed with a V-lock stitch.
